reasoning: Black piedra is a minor superficial fungal infection of the hair, treated simply with antifungal shampoo or shaving. There are currently no approved fungal vaccines for humans at all, and a trivial condition like black piedra has zero economic or medical incentive to warrant vaccine development. The Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' currently lists no vaccine. The only conceivable way this could resolve 'Yes' is if the Wikipedia page is vandalized or erroneously edited on the exact resolution date to list a vaccine. Because this page is monitored and the disease is obscure, the chance of this happening and persisting precisely on those dates is astronomically low. Consequently, I assign probabilities at the lowest allowable floor, increasing only very slightly over time to account for the infinitesimally greater opportunity for Wikipedia anomalies over longer horizons.
